name: Cold Traffic Article Pre-Frame Funnel

goals: Warm up COLD, non-problem-aware traffic with a native article before any opt-in or offer.
Bridge the cold audience's existing beliefs to your solution using stories written in THEIR language, then capture the email.
Feed leads into a 30-60 day cold-to-warm nurture before any direct purchase ask.
signals: Traffic is cold and unaware of the problem/solution (e.g. broad interest-targeted ads, content/native placements).
Direct-response sales pages convert poorly because the audience isn't ready.
Client has a longer follow-up sequence and patience for a multi-week nurture.
antiSignals: Warm/hot traffic that already knows the problem — send them straight to a sales/VSL funnel.
No nurture sequence in place — the article captures leads that then go cold.

pageStructure.purpose: Read as native editorial content (not an ad); meet the cold reader at their awareness level, tell relevant stories, and bridge their beliefs toward the solution.
elements: Editorial-style headline in the audience's own language (curiosity, not pitch)
Story lead (Epiphany Bridge) that the cold reader sees themselves in
Belief-bridging body: reframe the vehicle/internal/external false beliefs
Native formatting (byline, article layout, images, subheads)
Soft in-content CTAs to the opt-in (no hard sell)

pageStructure.purpose: Convert the warmed reader with a lead magnet directly related to the article's promise.
elements: Lead magnet headline continuing the article's thread

pageStructure.purpose: Deliver the lead magnet and explicitly enter the lead into the long cold-to-warm nurture.
elements: Set expectations for the email relationship (what's coming)
Optional low-friction next step (follow socials / watch a bridge video)

copyFramework.primaryPersona: Russell Brunson — Expert Secrets persona (The Attractive Character / Expert): Epiphany Bridge story script, the Big Domino, false-belief patterns (vehicle/internal/external), Perfect Webinar, The Stack and Close, Hook-Story-Offer.
supportingPersonas: Jim Edwards — Copywriting Secrets persona (The Copy Mechanic): the Big Promise headline formulas, fascination/curiosity bullets, the order-bump and OTO close formulas, the 'one-sentence' hook.

Donald Miller — StoryBrand persona (The Brand Clarifier): the SB7 framework — (1) a Character who (2) has a Problem, (3) meets a Guide, (4) who gives them a Plan, (5) and calls them to Action, (6) that ends in Success and (7) helps them avoid Failure — plus the one-liner and the grunt test.

Russell Brunson — DotCom Secrets persona (The Funnel Architect): Star/Story/Solution sales letter script, the Value Ladder, Who/What/Why/How lead logic, Soap Opera Sequence + Daily Seinfeld follow-up.
scripts: Epiphany Bridge (Expert Secrets) written at the COLD audience's awareness level — their words, their world.
Advertorial / story-lead formula (Jim Edwards) so the page reads as editorial, not as an ad.
StoryBrand problem framing to name the villain/problem for non-problem-aware readers.
Soap Opera Sequence (DotCom Secrets) for the 30-60 day cold-to-warm nurture that follows.
copyFramework.notes: Do NOT pitch on the article. The job is belief-shift + opt-in. Write in the reader's vernacular and let stories do the persuading; the offer comes weeks later inside the nurture.

pages: Build as a GHL Funnel: Step 1 Advertorial article page -> Step 2 Opt-in page -> Step 3 Thank-you/delivery.
Style the article step with editorial CSS (byline, narrow column, images) so it reads native.
skill44Widgets: Skill 44 Form widget: email opt-in on Step 2 (lead magnet).
Skill 44 inline form/CTA block embedded mid-article for soft early opt-ins.
automations: Workflow: opt-in -> deliver lead magnet -> START 30-60 day cold-to-warm nurture (Soap Opera -> Daily Seinfeld).
No purchase ask until late in the sequence — gate the first offer behind engagement.
Compliance: label paid native placements as advertising where required.
ghlBuild.notes: Skill 6 builds the 3-step funnel and applies the editorial template to the article step; Skill 44 builds the opt-in form and the mid-article inline form. The nurture workflow is the heart of this funnel — budget the most effort there.

metrics: Cost per lead from cold traffic
